name: Ensure disks are not publicly accessible
description: Disks attached to virtual machines that expose SSH or RDP to the internet.
body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when a disk is attached to a VM that has a public IP and inbound SSH or RDP open to the internet. The disk itself may not be directly public, but the attached VM creates an internet path to the data on that disk.

Remove public management access or detach sensitive disks from publicly reachable workloads.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Fix the public management path to the VM that uses the affected disk.

1. Identify the VM attached to the affected disk.
2. Remove public IP access if the workload does not need to be internet-facing.
3. Remove inbound SSH/RDP rules from `0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`, Internet tags, or empty source rules.
4. Use bastion, VPN, private endpoints, or identity-aware access for administration.
5. If the disk contains sensitive data and the VM must remain public, move the disk/data to a private workload.

Provider examples:

```bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress --group-id <security-group-id> --protocol tcp --port 22 --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 --region {{asset.region}}
az network nsg rule delete --resource-group <resource-group> --nsg-name <nsg-name> --name <rule-name>
gcloud compute firewall-rules update <rule-name> --source-ranges=<trusted-cidr>
```

Cyscale will pass when the VM attached to the disk is no longer publicly reachable on SSH or RDP.
